summary: Intimate club show with fiery The Other One and strong Rhythm Devils
blurb: Features a scorching The Other One that rides a tight Rhythm Devils > Phil bottleneck segment, plus solid renditions of Althea, Wharf Rat and the debut of Spoonful. Stripped‑down instrumentation gives the show a raw, garage‑level feel while the band stays energetic throughout both sets.
review: On October 15 1981 the Dead slipped into Club Melk Weg for a secret, stripped‑down gig that feels like a house party in Amsterdam. Jerry led the way on a Yamaha‑style Strat, cutting through the intimate mix and driving the band especially during the fiery The Other One. Phil’s bottleneck solo threaded perfectly into the Rhythm Devils > Phil Bottleneck > The Other One flow, giving the drums—Mickey and Billy—a chance to shine with tight, energetic interplay. Bob kept his Telecaster lean and focused while Brent’s borrowed mini‑keyboard added a raw edge that suited the garage vibe. Highlights include Althea, Wharf Rat, a first‑time Spoonful (though buried under noisy chatter), and a relaxed second set that rolls from Man Smart, Woman Smarter into a smooth Johnny B. Goode finale.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive and one mixed, with consistent praise for The Other One, the Rhythm Devils > Phil bottleneck sequence, and the intimate raw feel. No negative comments about performance quality appear, so a strong positive sentiment adjustment of +1.6 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good audience tape that captures the raw, intimate vibe and features strong performances of The Other One and Wharf Rat with clear enough sound to enjoy the set.

key_highlights: Hot The Other One that rides a powerful Rhythm Devils > Phil Bottleneck > The Other One sequence.
Intimate, stripped‑down sound with Jerry on a Yamaha‑style Strat and Brent’s raw keyboard adding garage‑level edge.
First performance of Spoonful adds a rare moment despite noisy audience.

band_performance.Jerry: Jerry delivered solid lead work throughout both sets, his Yamaha‑style Strat cutting through the intimate mix and driving the energy, especially during The Other One.
band_performance.Phil: Phil provided a standout bottleneck solo that threaded into the Rhythm Devils > Phil Bottleneck > The Other One flow, adding soaring momentum to the drum exchange.
band_performance.Bob: Bob kept it tight on Telecaster, fitting the stripped‑down vibe and supporting the jams without overplaying.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s minimalist borrowed keyboard added a raw edge that complemented the garage feel of the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey helped anchor the powerful Rhythm Devils segment with energetic fills that propelled the band forward.
band_performance.Billy: Billy locked in with Mickey during the drums, delivering tight interplay that fueled the The Other One transition.
